label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user is asking about what we know about garage sales based on the provided passage. Let's go through the text again to make sure I don't miss any details. First, the passage mentions that garage sales are usually held in the spring and fall when the weather is still good. So the first thing I note is that they're not only in spring. That might relate to option A, which says "only held in the spring of the year." Since the passage says "usually in the spring and fall," option A is incorrect because it's not exclusive to spring. Next, the passage says that the homeowner is clearing out accumulated items that are no longer useful to him. The goods on display are in good condition and can be great buys if you have time and patience. So option B, which claims the goods are "totally useless," is incorrect because the text says they're in good condition and useful enough to be sold. Option C states that garage sales are popular with Americans. The passage starts by talking about flea markets being very popular and then transitions to saying "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." That strongly suggests that garage sales are popular, so option C is likely correct. Option D says "All the goods are selling far below their real value." The passage mentions that if the owner must sell the home or plans to move soon, they may give things away at a price far below true worth. However, this is a specific situation
